nodes.name: Schedule Trigger
nodes.notes: Triggers the LeakWatch ML analysis pipeline daily at 06:00 UTC. Adjust the cron expression for different schedules (e.g., '0 */6 * * *' for every 6 hours).

nodes.name: Run Analysis
nodes.notes: POST /api/v1/ml/run-analysis — Triggers a background ML scoring run. Returns { run_id, status: 'running' }. Requires admin-role JWT. Body can include optional filters: start_date, end_date, department.

nodes.name: Wait Before Poll
nodes.notes: Pauses 30 seconds between each status poll to give the ML pipeline time to process. Forms part of the polling loop (max 20 iterations = 10 min ceiling).

nodes.name: Check Run Status
nodes.notes: GET /api/v1/ml/run-status/{run_id} — Polls the analysis run status. Returns: run_id, status ('running'|'completed'|'failed'), transactions_scored, high_risk_count, duplicates_found.

nodes.name: Track Poll Count
nodes.notes: Merges the HTTP status response with a poll iteration counter. On first pass the self-reference throws, so pollCount defaults to 1. Prevents infinite loops by feeding poll_count to the Max Retries check downstream.

nodes.name: Is Analysis Complete?
nodes.notes: Branches on status == 'completed'. TRUE → evaluate results. FALSE → check if max retries reached.

nodes.name: Max Retries Reached?
nodes.notes: Safety guard: if poll_count >= 20 (10 minutes elapsed), stop polling and fire a timeout alert. FALSE loops back to Wait Before Poll for another 30-second cycle.

nodes.name: Has Anomalies?
nodes.notes: Checks if high_risk_count > 0. TRUE → fetch detail and alert. FALSE → no action needed.

nodes.name: Fetch High Risk Transactions
nodes.notes: GET /api/v1/dashboard/high-risk-transactions — Retrieves detailed high-risk transaction records including voucher numbers, leak probabilities, risk factors, and duplicate flags.

nodes.name: Format Alert Message
nodes.notes: Builds a human-readable alert message with run summary and up to 10 top high-risk transactions. Output fields: text (formatted message), summary (one-liner), top_transactions (array).

nodes.name: Send Alert Notification
nodes.notes: Sends alert via webhook (Slack, Discord, Teams, or custom). Set the ALERT_WEBHOOK_URL environment variable in n8n. Replace this node with an Email Send, Slack, or Teams node for native integration.

nodes.name: No Anomalies Detected
nodes.notes: No high-risk transactions found. Workflow ends cleanly.

nodes.name: Send Timeout Alert
nodes.notes: Fires if the analysis run does not complete within 20 × 30s = 10 minutes. Notifies ops team to investigate the backend.
